𝗕𝗨𝗭𝗭 | Offline audio and video transcription and translation on a personal computer.  🔥 Super Hot!

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Offline audio and video transcription and translation on a personal computer.
📅 Created: Sep 2022 • Updated: Apr 2026

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Transcribe audio and video files or Youtube links
✅ Live realtime audio transcription from microphone
✅ Speech separation before transcription
✅ Speaker identification
✅ Multiple whisper backend support (CUDA, Apple Silicon, Vulkan)
✅ Export transcripts to TXT, SRT, and VTT
✅ Advanced Transcription Viewer
✅ Watch folder for automatic transcription
✅ Command-Line Interface


✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 18.8K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 1.4K 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 22 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Buzz is a desktop application that transcribes and translates audio and video files offline on your personal computer, powered by OpenAI's Whisper. It offers advanced features like live transcription, speech separation, speaker identification, and multiple backend support for GPU acceleration.

✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧✧

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/chidiwilliams/buzz

𝗪𝗔𝗬𝗕𝗔𝗖𝗞-𝗠𝗔𝗖𝗛𝗜𝗡𝗘-𝗪𝗘𝗕𝗘𝗫𝗧𝗘𝗡𝗦𝗜𝗢𝗡 | To allow web browser users to easily access, view, and contribute to the historical archive of web pages maintained by the Internet Archive's Wayback Machine.  ✨ Notable!

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📜 JavaScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To allow web browser users to easily access, view, and contribute to the historical archive of web pages maintained by the Internet Archive's Wayback Machine.
📅 Created: Jan 2017 • Updated: Apr 2026

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Save Page Now (auto-save options)
✅ View Oldest, Newest & Overview of archived pages
✅ Wayback Machine Count display
✅ 404 Not Found error handling with archive check
✅ Contextual Notices (fact-checking, origin websites)
✅ Relevant Resources (Wikipedia, Amazon Books, TV News)
✅ URLs & Collections listing
✅ Site Map & Word Cloud generation
✅ Annotations via Hypothes.is
✅ My Web Archive integration
✅ Tweets & Share Links functionality


▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 807 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 230 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 57 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

This web browser extension provides direct access to the Internet Archive's Wayback Machine, allowing users to view historical versions of web pages, save current pages, and receive contextual information. It aims to preserve web history and provide users with tools to explore and understand how URLs have evolved over time.

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/internetarchive/wayback-machine-webextension

𝗦𝗥𝗧-𝗧𝗥𝗔𝗡𝗦𝗠𝗜𝗧𝗧𝗘𝗥 | Managing and monitoring multiple SRT (Secure Reliable Transport) streams through a desktop graphical user interface.  

❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📘 TypeScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Managing and monitoring multiple SRT (Secure Reliable Transport) streams through a desktop graphical user interface.
📅 Created: Jun 2025 • Updated: Jun 2025

❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Stream Management (Start, Stop, Monitor)
✅ Bulk Operations for Streams
✅ Real-time Log Viewing
✅ URL Copying
✅ Dynamic Stream Addition/Removal
✅ macOS-inspired UI with Dark/Light Mode
✅ Server IP Management
✅ Persistent Settings Storage
✅ Built-in srt-live-transmit binary support
✅ Cross-platform compatibility (macOS, Windows, Linux)


❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

🍴 1 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 0 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

SRT Stream Manager is a cross-platform desktop application built with Electron, React, and TypeScript for managing multiple SRT streams. It offers a user-friendly interface to start, stop, monitor, and configure streams, with features like bulk operations, real-time logs, and persistent storage.

❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀

𝗙𝗔𝗦𝗧𝗛𝗧𝗧𝗣 | Making asynchronous HTTP requests in Python with enhanced logging, type safety, and middleware capabilities.  🔥 Super Hot!

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Making asynchronous HTTP requests in Python with enhanced logging, type safety, and middleware capabilities.
📅 Created: Feb 2026 • Updated: Apr 2026

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Simple API with decorators
✅ Beautiful and colorful request/response logging
✅ Async support built on httpx
✅ Type safety with Pydantic support
✅ Support for all HTTP methods
✅ Middleware for request/response interception
✅ Rate limiting strategies
✅ Optional HTTP/2 protocol support
✅ Access to request details from response object


▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐ 27 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 1 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 0 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Fasthttp is a Python library that provides an asynchronous HTTP client with features like expressive logging, route-based requests, and support for HTTP/2. It simplifies making HTTP requests with a clean API, decorators, and middleware for request/response interception.

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/ndugram/fasthttp

𝗧𝗥𝗔𝗬-𝗥𝗔𝗜𝗡 | To provide users with a quick, at-a-glance view of local weather conditions, specifically temperature and the probability of rain, directly from their Windows system tray without needing to open a full application.  

♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide users with a quick, at-a-glance view of local weather conditions, specifically temperature and the probability of rain, directly from their Windows system tray without needing to open a full application.
📅 Created: Mar 2026 • Updated: Apr 2026

♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Displays current temperature in system tray
✅ Indicates expected rain for the day
✅ Map-based location picker
✅ Configurable settings (timezone, refresh interval, rain threshold)
✅ Builds a no-console Windows executable


♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐ 2 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 0 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 0 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Tray Rain is a small, amateur Windows desktop application that displays the current temperature and the likelihood of rain for the day directly from the system tray. It allows users to select their location using a map-based picker and configures settings like timezone and update intervals via a JSON file.

♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦♦
